title: Contemplanys Hermi
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg|italics=1}} {{Youmay|the clause in the Galactic Republic constitution|title=|the Baragwin [[Hermi Odle]]}} {{Law |relatedevents=*[[First Alsakan Conflict]]''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' *[[Corellian independence movement]]{{HoloNetNewsWeb|47|news/13314_1.html|Corellia Closes Borders}} *[[Separatist Crisis/Legends|Separatist Crisis]] *[[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]''[[Suns of Fortune]]'' |proposedby=[[Garm Bel Iblis/Legends|Garm Bel Iblis]]
{{C|second invocation}} |image=[[File:YoungBelIblis.jpg]] |name=''Contemplanys Hermi'' |dateproposed=*c. [[16,700 BBY]] {{C|creation}} *[[470 BBY]] {{C|first invocation}}''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the [[Corellian independence movement|Corellian attempt]] to become independent from the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] to [[470 BBY]]. *[[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]] {{C|second invocation}}{{HoloNetNewsWeb|47|news/13314_1.html|Corellia Closes Borders}} is dated to 13:3:14 of the [[Great ReSynchronization]] which translates to [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]] per the reasoning [[Template:YearMathGRS|here]]. |government=[[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] |legislativebody=[[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]] |result=[[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]] and its constituencies are able to unilaterally withdraw from Senate duties |side1=22 BBY: *Garm Bel Iblis
{{C|[[Senator/Legends|Senator]]; Corellia}} *[[Shyla Merricope]]
{{C|[[Diktat]]; Corellia}} |side2=22 BBY: *[[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]]
{{C|[[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]]}} *[[Orn Free Taa/Legends|Orn Free Taa]]
{{C|Senator; [[Ryloth/Legends|Ryloth]]}} *[[Ister Paddie/Legends|Ister Paddie]]
{{C|Senator; [[Sermeria]]}} |otherkeyindividuals= }} {{Quote|As Bel Iblis said, no Articles of Secession were tendered. As the official record stands, the Corellian delegation enacted an obscure proviso called ''Contemplanys Hermi'', an olys Corellisi phrase meaning 'meditative solitude.'|"Corellia Closes Borders," HoloNet News|}} '''''Contemplanys Hermi''''' was a clause in the [[Galactic Constitution/Legends|Galactic Constitution]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] that applied only to the [[Corellian sector/Legends|Corellian sector]] and its constituencies, which was home to one of the [[Core Founder/Legends|Core Founders]], [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]]. ''Contemplanys Hermi'' allowed for Corellia to close its borders and recall its [[Senator/Legends|delegation]] from the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]], thereby surrendering its veto power while remaining inside the Republic. The enactment of the proviso carried several limitations on non-[[Corellian/Legends|Corellians]] within the [[Sector/Legends|sector]], and also prevented the Republic from calling on the people of Corellia and the [[Corellian Security Force]] to contribute to the [[Republic Military/Legends|Republic Military]]. It was included in the Constitution in acknowledgment of Corellia's contributions toward founding the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] government. The clause was a proviso added in approximately [[16,700 BBY]] and was so rarely invoked that it was considered obscure. One recorded instance was Corellia's [[Corellian independence movement|attempted secession]] in [[470 BBY]]. In a much later incident, Corellian [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Garm Bel Iblis/Legends|Garm Bel Iblis]] claimed ''Contemplanys Hermi'' during the [[Separatist Crisis/Legends|Separatist Crisis]] so as to remove himself and his sector from the debate surrounding the [[Military Creation Act/Legends|Military Creation Act]], which Bel Iblis believed would threaten Corellian sovereignty. Senator Bel Iblis was highly criticized for the move, but he maintained his stance against the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]] throughout the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] and continued to neglect attending regular sessions of the Galactic Senate until compelled to do so during the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial Period]]. ==Description== {{Quote|CorSec's men and women will not be drafted into Republic service, to die on a distant world outside of Corellia's borders. Nor will armed forces from other worlds be billeted in our homes. ''[…]'' I will do whatever possible to preserve the integrity of Corellia for Corellians.|Corellian Diktat Shyla Merricope|}} ''Contemplanys Hermi'' was a proviso included in the [[Galactic Constitution/Legends|Galactic Constitution]], the document that established the laws and principles by which the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic's]] government would operate.''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' The term ''Contemplanys Hermi'' was a phrase in [[Olys Corellisi/Legends|Olys Corellisi]] that, when translated to [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]], meant "meditative solitude." The proviso was aptly named, as its invocation allowed for the [[Corellian sector/Legends|Corellian sector]] to close its borders and adopt a stance of neutrality while maintaining its standing as a member of the Republic—it was noted that ''Contemplanys Hermi'' was not an act of secession, but rather a declaration of suspended [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomatic]] relations. It could be invoked by the [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] delegation in the Republic's [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]], which would result in the delegation's withdrawal from Senate proceedings. As such, Corellia surrendered its veto power for the duration of ''Contemplanys Hermi''. This clause applied only to the Corellian [[Sector/Legends|sector]] and could be used by no other delegation. While the clause was in effect, the Corellian sector was inaccessible to all except for native and naturalized citizens of the region. For those to whom Corellia was not home, special permission was needed from the [[Corellian Security Force]] for travel into and out of the sector. Trade and commerce [[Starship/Legends|vessels]] were required to unload their cargoes on [[Outlier systems]], where they would then be retrieved by [[Transport/Legends|transports]] of the [[Corellian Merchants' Guild/Legends|Corellian Merchants' Guild]] and brought to the inner [[Planet/Legends|worlds]]. In addition, all off-duty and reserve members of the Corellian Security Force were activated during ''Contemplanys Hermi''. This also barred the Republic from absorbing members of the CSF into an interplanetary defense force for the preservation of the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] government and prohibited the government from stationing [[Infantry/Legends|soldiers]] from such a defense force on Corellian worlds. Despite the restrictions on non-native and non-naturalized citizens, non-Corellian businesses that were located within the sector were allowed to continue operating under the stipulation that they abide by the standing travel and shipping restrictions. To be in full compliance, these businesses usually had to purchase membership in the Corellian Merchants' Guild. ==History== ===Origins=== {{Quote|The Corellian sector, one of the founding fathers of the Republic, is not seceding. However, Corellia will not participate in what it deems a profound error that threatens its sovereignty.|Senator Garm Bel Iblis of Corellia|}} [[File:Corellia.png|thumb|left|200px|Corellia]] The planet Corellia and its respective sector played a pivotal role in founding the Galactic Republic in [[25,053 BBY]].''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic Encyclopedia]]'' dates the founding of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] to [[25,053 BBY]]. In recognition of its distinct society and contributions to the Republic, the [[Core Founder/Legends|Core Founder]] was afforded the ''Contemplanys Hermi'' clause—put into the constitution after the [[First Alsakan Conflict]] in approximately [[16,700 BBY]]. The reasons Corellia cited for the clause; another inevitable war between fellow [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core]] planets [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] and [[Alsakan/Legends|Alsakan]], and sympathy with the latter but having deep ties to the [[Grand Companies]] and the [[Corellian Run/Legends|Corellian Run]]. The clause allowed Corellia to separate itself from the affairs of the Republic for a period of [[Time/Legends|time]] including if Coruscant and Alsakan resumed hostilities. The clause rarely came into use and eventually fell into obscurity, but was brought to the forefront of galactic events when, in [[470 BBY]], Corellia made a [[Corellian independence movement|bid to become an independent governmental body]]. The attempt ultimately failed, and ''Contemplanys Hermi'' was largely forgotten. ===Separatist Crisis=== It would not be mentioned again until the [[Separatist Crisis/Legends|Separatist Crisis]] in the latter days of the Galactic Republic, during which a number of [[Star system/Legends|star systems]] and sectors declared their intention to secede from what they considered to be the "corrupt" Republic and form a new [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|government]] based on open market capitalism and limited interference from the interstellar state.{{DB|organization|cis|Confederacy of Independent Systems}} During this period, the Galactic Senate of the Republic began debating the [[Military Creation Act/Legends|Military Creation Act]], an initiative to create an [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Army of the Republic]] to defend the government should the growing independence movement ever turn violent.''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' From the time of the announcement of the Military Creation Act's vote date, the people of Corellia were critical of the legislation, particularly those in the planet's [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Coronet City/Legends|Coronet]]. In the Galactic Congress, Corellian [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Garm Bel Iblis/Legends|Garm Bel Iblis]] was among the most outspoken opponents of the act, believing that it was not only the wrong course for the Republic, but for his constituents as well. After a [[Standard week/Legends|week]] of closed-door meetings between the Senator and Corellian [[Diktat]] [[Shyla Merricope]], Bel Iblis returned to the Senate on 3:14 in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]] and invoked ''Contemplanys Hermi'', thereby pulling his delegation out of the debate over the Military Creation Act and insulating his sector from its outcome. Historically, ''Contemplanys Hermi'' was a measure that had caused the rest of the Senate to reconsider actions that Corellians thought perilous. Ultimately Bel Iblis's gamble did not work and the Military Creation Act was passed and the galaxy was torn apart by war. During his address to the Senate on the matter, Bel Iblis reassured his colleagues that no [[Articles of Secession]] were tendered by the Corellian government and that the invocation was not a step towards joining the Separatist movement. On Corellia, Diktat Merricope told [[Corellia Sector Newsfeed]] that the move was intended to protect the people of Corellia and to preserve the sector for its natives. In response to the news, [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] issued an offer of parley to Confederate leader [[Count/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]], which ultimately fell on deaf ears. Corellia remained a neutral sector for the remainder of the Separatist Crisis and continued its isolationist stance into the resultant [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. ===Post-Clone Wars=== Shortly after the end of the Clone Wars and the establishment of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] with Chancellor Palpatine's [[Declaration of a New Order]], Corellia's "meditative solitude" was brought to an end when Senator Bel Iblis was called upon to attend meetings of the [[Imperial Senate/Legends|Imperial Senate]]''[[Dark Lord: The Rise of Darth Vader]]'' after Emperor Palpatine struck the proviso from the Constitution as an ancient relic. Future intergalactic governments would prove to be less tolerant of Corellian isolationism than the Republic had been, signaling an end to the special recognition granted to the sector that had been embodied in ''Contemplanys Hermi''. The seclusion of the Corellian sector in the post-Palpatine era was actively opposed by [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[Chief of State (New Republic)/Legends|Chief of State]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]], and the isolation movement of [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]] was illegal under the provisions of the [[Galactic Alliance's common charter|Galactic Alliance Common Charter]].''[[Legacy of the Force: Betrayal]]'' The latter instance ultimately led to the [[Second Galactic Civil War]] in an effort to end Corellia's isolationist tendencies.''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' ==Controversy== {{Quote|This is an outrageous display of self-serving hypocrisy. Two years ago, Bel Iblis stood before the Senate, arguing about the legality of secession, and now he goes and pulls a stunt like this? It's a grievous blow to unity. My worlds, in the distant Outer Rim have stood by Coruscant through the most tumultuous of heat storms. That one so integral to the Core should lack a spine is shameful.|Senator Orn Free Taa of Ryloth|}} [[File:Orn_free_taa.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Senator Orn Free Taa, one of Bel Iblis's most vocal critics]] Upon Senator Bel Iblis's declaration of ''Contemplanys Hermi'' in 22 BBY, he was met with harsh criticism from his colleagues in the Senate. He and his delegation departed the [[Grand Convocation Chamber|Senate Convocation Chamber]] amid cries of protest and had to be escorted from the [[Senate Building/Legends|Senate Dome]] by Corellian Security Personnel and [[Senate Guard/Legends|Senate Guards]] as they pushed through [[Journalist/Legends|journalists]] and outraged representatives. The upheaval was yet another blow to the already crumbling Galactic Republic. Supreme Chancellor Palpatine, to whom Bel Iblis was commonly a [[Politics/Legends|political]] opponent, was attending a financial conference on [[Aargau/Legends|Aargau]] during the session, but did release a statement regarding the Corellian delegation's move. In the statement, he expressed his disappointment that a sector so close to the heart of the Republic lacked faith that the democratic vote on the Military Creation Act would yield a positive outcome for the greater galaxy. [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]]{{DB|character|ornfreetaa|Taa, Orn Free}} Senator [[Orn Free Taa/Legends|Orn Free Taa]] of [[Ryloth/Legends|Ryloth]] was less measured in his response to [[HoloNet News/Legends|HoloNet News]] reporters, branding the invocation as hypocrisy and citing Bel Iblis's statements two [[Standard year/Legends|years]] [[24 BBY/Legends|prior]] regarding the legality of secession. The Twi'lek went on to laud [[Gaulus sector/Legends|his sector's]] loyalty to the Republic through many tumultuous periods and decried Corellia's lack of similar fidelity as "shameful." [[Human/Legends|Human]]{{DB|character|isterpaddie|Paddie, Ister}} Senator [[Ister Paddie/Legends|Ister Paddie]] of [[Sermeria]] echoed Free Taa's sentiment, condemning ''Contemplanys Hermi'' as a selfish act, given that Corellia was one of the wealthiest sectors in the Republic and totally self-sufficient. Paddie stressed that his constituent sector of [[Locris sector/Legends|Locris]]{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} was far less affluent, but still remained committed to supporting the Republic in its hour of crisis. ==Behind the scenes== ''Contemplanys Hermi'' was first introduced in [[HoloNet News Vol. 531 47]], a [[2002]] update to the [[HoloNet News (original website)|HoloNet News website]]. HoloNet News was a regular online feature preceding the release of [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Star Wars'': Episode II ''Attack of the Clones'']]. The article regarding ''Contemplanys Hermi'', "Corellia Closes Borders," was part of a larger narrative that took place throughout HoloNet News regarding the debate over the Military Creation Act. ==Appearances== *{{HoloNetNewsWeb|47|news/13314_1.html|Corellia Closes Borders}} {{1st}} ==Sources== *''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' *''[[Suns of Fortune]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Contemplanys Hermi |es=Contemplanys Hermi |hu=Contemplanys Hermi }} [[Category:Alsakan Conflicts laws and acts]] [[Category:Amendments to the Galactic Constitution]] [[Category:Corellian culture]] [[Category:Separatist Crisis laws and acts]]
